title: Big Tech earnings fuels mixed stock moves, as Apple pops, Meta plunges, and Alphabet surges
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Big Tech earnings led to mixed stock moves: Apple surged, Meta plunged, and Alphabet rose significantly. AI spending plans drove the market reactions.
excerpt: Big Tech earnings led to mixed stock moves: Apple surged, Meta plunged, and Alphabet rose significantly. AI spending plans drove the market reactions.

content.introduction: Technology sector earnings reports created significant market volatility last week, with major tech giants reporting divergent financial results. Apple, Meta, and Alphabet each moved markets in distinct directions as investors assessed their financial health and strategic priorities, particularly concerning artificial intelligence investments.
body:
## Apple Surges on Strong iPhone Sales and Service Growth

Apple Inc. (AAPL) exceeded market expectations with its fiscal second-quarter earnings report, sending shares higher by approximately 4% in after-hours trading. The company reported revenue of $90.8 billion, slightly ahead of analyst forecasts of $90.4 billion, with iPhone sales reaching $51.3 billion, up 2.2% year-over-year. The strong performance was bolstered by robust demand for the iPhone 15 lineup and record-breaking services revenue of $23.9 billion, representing a 14.2% increase. CEO Tim Cook emphasized the company's expansion into emerging markets and the continued strength of its ecosystem, which includes 1.9 billion active devices. Analysts at Morgan Stanley noted that Apple's growing services segment provides a more predictable revenue stream, helping to offset potential volatility in hardware sales.

## Meta Faces Headwinds Despite Revenue Beat

Meta Platforms Inc. (META) experienced a significant stock drop of nearly 8% following its earnings report, despite revenue exceeding expectations. The company reported first-quarter revenue of $36.5 billion, up 27% year-over-year and ahead of the $36.1 billion consensus estimate. However, investors reacted negatively to Meta's guidance for the second quarter, which projected revenue between $36.5 billion and $39 billion, below the $39.5 billion analysts had anticipated. The company's Reality Labs division continued to post substantial losses, with $3.7 billion in operating expenses for the quarter. CFO Susan Li indicated that Meta would maintain its aggressive investment in AI and the metaverse, with capital expenditures expected to reach $40-45 billion for the full year. This spending commitment, while necessary for long-term growth, weighed on investor sentiment in the near term.

## Alphabet Benefits from AI Monetization

Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) delivered a standout performance with its first-quarter results, driving shares up more than 7% after hours. The parent company of Google reported revenue of $80.5 billion, a 15% increase year-over-year and above the $79.6 billion consensus. Google Cloud revenue grew 28% to $9.6 billion, with operating income reaching $900 million, marking a significant improvement from the previous year. CEO Sundar Pichai highlighted the company's progress in monetizing its AI capabilities through the Gemini model and integration across search and advertising products. The company also announced a $70 billion share repurchase program, providing additional confidence to investors. According to analysts at Goldman Sachs, Alphabet's position in the generative AI space positions it favorably for continued revenue growth, particularly as businesses increasingly adopt AI-powered advertising solutions.
keyTakeaways: Big Tech earnings led to mixed stock reactions, with Apple rising, Meta falling, and Alphabet surging. The mixed results
Meta, Alphabet, Amazon, and Microsoft all beat earnings estimates, but the stocks had mixed reactions. Here's why

content.outlook:
Looking ahead, The situation paints a bigger bullseye on AI capex. Magnificent 7 companies have pledged tens of billions in spending, but market reactions have been inconsistent. What traders really care about is AI progress, and they favor companies that most quickly convert that investment into results.

The new. Market participants will continue to monitor developments closely for signals about the direction of company news trends and their broader implications for portfolio allocation and risk management strategies.
